items.text:
Subject: Urban Sketching Walk – This Saturday!

Hi everyone,

Are you looking for a creative way to explore campus? The Art Club is hosting an Urban Sketching Walk this Saturday, May 15, from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM. We'll meet at the fountain in front of Anderson Hall.

No experience is necessary, and all materials are provided for a $5.00 fee. Just bring your enthusiasm and comfortable walking shoes! We'll sketch several campus landmarks, including the old library and the sculpture garden. Our guide, Ms. Elena Rossi, is a professional illustrator who'll share tips on perspective and shading.

Space is limited to 25 participants, so please register by Friday, May 14, at 5:00 PM on the Art Club's portal. You can pay the fee online or bring cash to the event. If the weather is bad, we'll move the walk indoors to the Student Union.

Best regards,

Jamie Park
Art Club President

questions.stem: According to the email, what must participants pay?

options.B: A $2.00 fee for a guide booklet

questions.explanation: The email states, 'all materials are provided for a $5.00 fee.'

questions.stem: What must interested students do by May 14 at 5:00 PM?
questions.explanation: The email says, 'please register by Friday, May 14, at 5:00 PM on the Art Club's portal.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Urban Sketching Walk?

options.A: It requires participants to bring their own sketchbooks
options.B: It will be canceled if fewer than 10 people register
options.C: It may be held inside if the weather is poor
options.D: It offers a discount for early registration

questions.explanation: The email states, 'If the weather is bad, we'll move the walk indoors to the Student Union,' implying an indoor alternative exists.

items.text:
Subject: Join the Spring Festival Planning Committee!

Dear Students,

Are you passionate about campus events? The Student Activities Office invites you to join the Spring Festival Planning Committee. We're organizing this year's festival, which will take place on April 20 from 11:00 AM to 6:00 PM on the Main Quad.

Committee members will help with decorations, music scheduling, and food vendor coordination. Meetings are held every Wednesday at 4:00 PM in Room 204 of the Student Union. Attendance at all meetings is expected, but we understand if you miss one due to class conflicts.

To apply, please fill out the online form by March 1. You'll need to list your availability and any relevant experience. Selected members will receive a free festival T-shirt and a $10 meal voucher. We're looking for 15 dedicated students, so don't wait!

Thanks for your interest,

Maria Chen
Student Activities Coordinator

format_metadata.subject: Join the Spring Festival Planning Committee!

options.B: Submit a list of food vendors
options.D: Pay a $10 deposit for the T-shirt

items.text:
Subject: Film Screening: The Art of Animation

Hi Film Club Members,

We're excited to announce our next event: a screening of "The Art of Animation" this Friday, March 10, at 7:30 PM in the Johnson Auditorium. This documentary explores the history of animation from classic hand-drawn cells to modern CGI.

Tickets are $2.00 for Film Club members and $5.00 for non-members. You can purchase them at the door or in advance at the Student Union information desk. Seating is first-come, first-served, and the auditorium holds 120 people. We recommend arriving early to get a good spot!

After the screening, there will be a Q&A session with Professor Kim from the Film Studies Department, who consulted on the documentary. This is a great chance to ask questions and learn more about the animation industry.

Hope to see you there!

Liam O'Brien
Film Club Treasurer

format_metadata.subject: Film Screening: The Art of Animation

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the cost of a ticket for a Film Club member?

options.B: $2.00 in advance or at the door

questions.explanation: The email says, 'Tickets are $2.00 for Film Club members... You can purchase them at the door or in advance.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the seating at the screening?

options.A: Reserved seats are available for members
options.B: Latecomers may not find a place to sit

questions.explanation: The email states, 'Seating is first-come, first-served' and 'the auditorium holds 120 people,' implying that arriving late could mean no seat.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To invite members to a film screening and Q&A session
options.B: To announce a change in the Film Club meeting time
options.C: To request volunteers for the event setup
options.D: To promote a new documentary course

questions.explanation: The email primarily announces and invites members to the screening and the Q&A session.

rejected.reason: answer_position: all 3 answers are "B" — must vary
